title: MigrationToken (v1)
description: Relay-signed authorization for an agent's migration. Issued by the source relay; consumed by the destination relay as proof the agent has authorization to leave. See spec/migration-v1.md §4.1.

token_id.description: UUIDv7 identifier for this specific authorization. Stable through expiry.

motebit_id.description: Agent's motebit identity (UUIDv7) authorized to migrate.

source_relay_url.description: Issuing relay's canonical URL. Lets destination relays reach back to verify chain-of-custody if needed.

issued_at.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the source relay signed the token.

expires_at.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the token becomes invalid. Default per spec: 72 hours from issuance. Source relay MUST NOT revoke before expiry except on agent-initiated cancellation (foundation law §4.3).

suite.description: Cryptosuite identifier. Always `motebit-jcs-ed25519-b64-v1` for migration artifacts: JCS canonicalization (RFC 8785), Ed25519 primitive, base64url signature, hex public key. Verifiers reject missing or unknown values fail-closed.

signature.description: Base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature over `canonicalJson(body)` where `body` = the artifact minus `signature`. Signed by the source relay (NOT the agent).
